---
title: "DARTER pitfalls"
subtitle: "Quirks and known issues specific to project 708421"
date: last-modified
---

This page supplements the [general DST pitfalls](../15c_dst_faldgruber.qmd) with issues specific to the DARTER project.

---

## 1. Check that parquet files are up to date {#1-check-that-parquet-files-are-up-to-date}

Most registers are as of 2026 updated to **end of 2024** (confirmed by Anders Aasted Isaksen/Marie Kempf Frydendahl, DARTER team).
**`dodsaars` covers only ~1970‚Äì2001** however ‚Äî deaths after 2001 are not captured by the current code.

```r
# Check when the parquet folder was last updated:
file.info("E:/workdata/708421/cleaned-data/parquet-registers/dodsaars/")$mtime
```

The same may apply to other registers. Always confirm that coverage matches your study period before running the pipeline.

::: {.callout-warning}
**If the parquet file does not cover your study period:**
You need to extract data from the raw SAS file on DST. Contact your data manager ‚Äî they can help with raw data access and conversion.
:::

```r
# Current code ‚Äî only catches deaths up to parquet file coverage:
death <- load_database("dodsaars") %>% rename_with(tolower)   # lazy connection
deaths <- death %>%
  filter(pnr %in% !!pnr_list) %>%   # only the cohort's pnr's
  select(pnr, d_dodsdto) %>%         # only death date
  collect()                           # fetch into R
```

**Consequence of missing coverage:** Comparators and BS patients who die after the parquet file's end date are treated as alive ‚Äî this affects censoring and matching in `01_build_cohorts.R`.

---

## 2. Surgery and procedures

Procedure codes are split across two registers by period:

- **`lpr_sksopr`** (parquet-registers) ‚Äî procedures and surgery 1996‚Äì2018, joined to `lpr_adm` via `recnum`
- **`procedurer_kirurgi`** (parquet-external) ‚Äî 2019 and onwards, joined to `lpr_a_kontakt` via `dw_ek_forloeb`

::: {.callout-warning}
**`dw_ek_kontakt` is NA for all rows in `procedurer_kirurgi`** (confirmed 2026-06-02).
Use `dw_ek_forloeb` ‚Äî not `dw_ek_kontakt` ‚Äî to fetch `pnr` from `lpr_a_kontakt`.
:::

```r
# WRONG ‚Äî dw_ek_kontakt is NA:
proc %>% left_join(contacts, by = "dw_ek_kontakt")   # joins nothing

# CORRECT ‚Äî use dw_ek_forloeb:
proc <- load_database("procedurer_kirurgi") %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  left_join(
    load_database("lpr_a_kontakt") %>%
      rename_with(tolower) %>%
      select(dw_ek_forloeb, pnr),
    by = "dw_ek_forloeb"
  )
```

---

## 3. `lpr_a_diagnose` ‚Äî "a" does not mean A-type diagnoses

The table is called `lpr_a_diagnose` ‚Äî "a" refers to the analysis model designation (LPR_A series).
It is **not** a filter on A-type diagnoses. The table contains A, B and G.
You still need to filter on `diag_kode_type`.

---

## 4. `nmi_count` ‚âÝ `nmi_score`

| Variable | What it is |
|---|---|
| `nmi_score` | **Weighted** score ‚Äî Nordic Multimorbidity Index (50 predictors with individual weights) |
| `nmi_count` | Simple **count** of the number of chronic conditions (33 possible) |

If you use `nmi_count` in your Cox model instead of `nmi_score`, you are adjusting for something different than you think.

---

## 5. LPR3 ‚Äî filter on `lprindberetningssystem == "LPR3"` {#lpr3-lprindberetningssystem}

The LPR_A registers (`lpr_a_kontakt`, `lpr_a_diagnose`) contain data from **two formats**: the old LPR_F and the new LPR_A. Both exist in the project and cover overlapping periods. Without the filter you get **duplicated rows** (confirmed by Anders Aasted Isaksen, DARTER team 2026).

```r
# CORRECT ‚Äî filter to LPR_A format only:
lpr3_k <- load_database("lpr_a_kontakt") %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  filter(lprindberetningssystem == "LPR3")   # removes LPR_F overlap and duplicates
```

::: {.callout-warning}
`get_lpr_diagnoses()` in `darter/00_index.qmd` is updated with this filter. If you have copies of LPR3 code in your own scripts, you must add it manually.
:::

---

## 6. Laboratory results ‚Äî use `laboratorieproevesvar_`

The new laboratory data register is called `laboratorieproevesvar_` and contains >2.2 billion rows.
The old `lab_forsker` / `lab_dm_forsker` still exists but covers the same data ‚Äî use **only one source** to avoid duplicates.

```r
lab <- load_database("laboratorieproevesvar_") %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  filter(pnr %in% !!kohort$pnr) %>%   # filter BEFORE collect ‚Äî the register is very large
  select(pnr, npu, samplingdato, samplevalue) %>%
  collect()
# samplevalue is character ‚Äî can contain "not detected", "negative" etc.
```

---

## See also

- [General DST pitfalls](../15c_dst_faldgruber.qmd) ‚Äî 10 pitfalls that apply to all projects
- [Register paths and datastores](01_registersti-datastores.qmd) ‚Äî confirmed paths and access methods
